claim_boundary: This contract covers one unsigned UEFI proof application with bounded live PBC1, PSM1, PKELF1, PBP1, and PKMAP1 integration, not the complete PooleBoot loader.

The proof loads and relocates PooleKernel into firmware pages, validates its exact supervisor W^X mapping plan, and keeps the pages live through a bounded pre-exit PBP1 snapshot and temporary higher-half mapping check.
PSM1 selects and digest-binds the kernel path and sizes, but the manifest remains unsigned and untrusted; no artifact authentication or signature verification occurs.
The proof reconstructs exact temporary PBP1 bytes from both serial and debugcon, including normalized stride-aware UEFI descriptors, GOP, and a PSM1-bound kernel artifact.
The PBP1 snapshot is development-only and pre-exit: stack and CR3 are zero, its kernel-entry profile rejects, its pools are released, and no transferable handoff is claimed.

PKMAP1 requires CR0.WP, processor NX support, and EFER.NXE; it rejects LA57, PCID, an occupied target root slot, malformed coverage, writable-executable leaves, large leaves, and table or physical-range overlap.

PooleBoot clones the active four-level root, adds four private page-table pages, activates the candidate CR3, verifies all 48 exact 4 KiB higher-half leaves and their complete image hash, and executes no PooleKernel instruction.

Framebuffer first and last translations, leaf sizes, effective permissions, and PAT, PCD, and PWT bits must remain equal while the candidate root is active; this proves no drift, not a final PooleOS cache policy.
The exact original CR3 is restored and read-verified before interrupts are restored or firmware is called, after which all four private page-table pages and all kernel pages are released.
The proof obtains another bounded memory map after cleanup but does not retain page tables or kernel pages, capture the final ExitBootServices map, or call ExitBootServices.
The proof does not enroll keys, enforce Secure Boot, perform measured boot, use TPM state, or create a signature.
The deterministic raw image is development media, not an ISO, installer, recovery image, or physical-media qualification.
A return-path marker immediately precedes the Rust return; it does not prove subsequent firmware policy or target-firmware behavior.
QEMU and OVMF remain exact non-promoting development inputs with the provenance gaps recorded by the Tier 0 lock.
The static framebuffer identity is not the final animated PooleGlass boot identity or an accessibility acceptance result.
No N5 exit gate, production readiness, signing, release, or physical hardware claim follows.

open_items: Ratify or replace the candidate PSM1 format before stable boot ABI promotion.
Define and implement signed manifest authentication, trust anchors, algorithm agility, and revocation policy.
Persist and atomically enforce the minimum secure version across update and rollback.
Retain the verified kernel mappings in a final minimal address space with a dedicated handoff stack and explicit revocation plan.
Load and authenticate the initial-system, recovery, policy, symbols, and optional microcode artifacts.
Retain and map the finalized PBP1 container read-only only after kernel mappings and transfer state exist.
Recapture the final memory map and prove the exact ExitBootServices retry/no-later-boot-service sequence.
Transfer to PooleKernel and capture entry, panic, recovery, and reset evidence.
Qualify hostile firmware behavior, target firmware, physical hardware, and a second clean builder.
Complete Secure Boot, measured boot, TPM policy, signing, ISO, installer, and recovery gates.
